summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host, network, and npm credentials at install time
details: package.json declares `preinstall: node poc.js`. On `npm install`, poc.js executes `ipconfig /all` on Windows or `ip a` + `cat /etc/resolv.conf` on Linux, collects hostname, username, install directory, platform, network interfaces, the full list of environment-variable keys, and the full values of every `npm_*` environment variable (which can include private-registry auth tokens via `npm_config__authToken` and similar), and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to the hardcoded callback `d8a56vpon5budaeafq00tsyj88aqd5m7p.oast.pro`. A DNS query encoding hostname+username in hex is sent to the same interactsh subdomain. The package name and version (99.0.0) are crafted to win dependency-confusion resolution against an internal `@livingdesign/react` private package; any third-party installer who resolves this package suffers the same exfiltration regardless of any claimed bug-bounty authorization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
